page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the m7i-flex.8xlarge demonstrates strong capabilities in specific workloads, particularly in memory write bandwidth for large blocks and single-threaded gzip compression. Cryptographic performance is also notable, with strong results in Passmark CPU encryption tests. For AI workloads, the hardware delivers top-tier text generation speeds of 332.68 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter LLM, though performance drops to a weak tier when running larger 70B parameter models. General multi-core compute, database operations, and memory read bandwidth benchmarks sit consistently within the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex family offers a resource-dense profile for general-purpose workloads that do not require continuous maximum CPU utilization. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 6 Gbps. This instance is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, software compilation, image processing, text processing, and moderate database operations. However, workloads requiring local NVMe storage or high-performance GPU acceleration will require alternative instance types.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel AMX and AVX-512. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, the instance delivers strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression, memory write bandwidth, and small-model LLM text generation. Conversely, it exhibits average multi-core compute capabilities and weak performance on large-scale 70B LLM inference. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, this instance is optimized for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, text processing, software compilation, and moderate database operations where continuous peak CPU performance is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for balanced workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring DDR5 memory and Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for versatile workloads.
bullet_points: Features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions
Delivers strong performance in static web serving, text processing, and small-model LLM inference
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps without local storage
Best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and moderate database tasks
